post.description: Generate a security audit for your n8n instance.

get.description:
"Retrieve the instance security policy: personal-space publishing and sharing, the execution-data redaction enforcement floor, and the read-only usage counts shown in the UI. Requires the `securitySettings:manage` scope and the Personal Space Policy feature to be licensed.
"

put.description: Replace the instance security policy with the provided full object. Every writable field must be sent. Read-only usage counts from GET are ignored if included, so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body. The update takes effect exactly as it would from the UI, using the same validation. Requires the `securitySettings:manage` scope and the Personal Space Policy feature to be licensed. When the group is managed via environment variables, the write is rejected with 409 and no changes are made; a read still returns the current values.

requestBody.description: The full security policy to set.

get.description: Retrieve the current SAML SSO configuration, including every field exposed in the UI plus the service provider entity ID and ACS return URL. Signing private keys, signing certificates, and identity provider metadata are redacted on read. Requires the `saml:manage` scope and the SAML feature to be licensed.

put.description: Replace the SAML SSO configuration with the provided full object. Every writable field must be sent; use empty strings or empty arrays when a value is unset. Read-only `entityID` / `returnUrl` from GET are ignored if included, so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body. Redacted secret placeholders keep the stored values unchanged. The update takes effect exactly as it would from the UI, using the same validation. Requires the `saml:manage` scope and the SAML feature to be licensed. When the configuration is managed via environment variables, the write is rejected with 409 and no changes are made.

requestBody.description: The full SAML SSO configuration to set.

post.summary: "Beta: Export workflows, folders, or projects as an n8n package"
post.description:
"**Beta** — breaking changes may still occur without major version bump.

Export workflows and/or folders, or projects, as a gzipped tar archive
(.n8np). Provide `workflowIds`/`folderIds`, or `projectIds`, but not both
groups. Each exported folder includes its nested folders. Empty projects
export project metadata only. Statically referenced sub-workflows must also
be included in the resulting package, otherwise the export is rejected. The
response is streamed as `application/gzip` with a `Content-Disposition`
attachment header. Requires the n8n Packages feature to be licensed.

API key scopes: `workflow:export` is required when exporting workflows or folders, and `project:export` is required when exporting projects. When `includeVariableValues` is true (the default) and the exported workflows reference variables, `variable:list` is also required; exports that reference no variables never need it.
"

requestBody.description: Workflows, folders, or projects to include in the exported package.

200.description: A gzipped tar archive containing the exported package contents.

post.summary: "Beta: Import an n8n package into a project"
post.description:
**Beta** — breaking changes may still occur without major version bump.

Imports a gzip-compressed tar package (`.n8np`) into the target project. Send the
archive as the multipart field `package`. Optional routing uses form fields
`projectId` and `folderId` (omit or send empty for defaults). The optional
`credentialMatchingMode`, `credentialMissingMode`, `bindings`,
`workflowIdPolicy`, and `workflowPublishingPolicy` fields take their defaults when omitted. The required
`workflowConflictPolicy` field controls what happens when a package workflow
matches an existing workflow by source id in the target project. Maximum upload
size is `N8N_ENDPOINTS_PAYLOAD_SIZE_MAX` MB (default 16).

The package must declare its manifest at `manifest.json` and include every file
referenced by the manifest. The caller is authorised through the `workflow:import`
scope. With the default `workflowIdPolicy=new`, newly created workflows receive a
fresh local id; imported workflows arrive inactive unless `workflowPublishingPolicy`
publishes them after import.

Credential references are resolved before any workflow is written. Credentials owned by,
shared with, or global to the target project (with `credential:read`) match.

200.description: Import succeeded; the listed workflows were written to the target project.

409.description: Import blocked by at least one conflict among the issues — a workflow source-id conflict or a folder conflict (id under a different parent, id owned by another project, or a `fail`-policy match).

422.description: Import blocked by non-conflict issues only (e.g. unresolved credentials).

get.description: List the event types that can be streamed to a log streaming destination. Requires the `eventBusDestination:list` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed.
List the configured log streaming destinations. Requires the `eventBusDestination:list` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed.

post.description: Create a log streaming destination. The destination takes effect exactly as it would from the UI, using the same validation. Requires the `eventBusDestination:create` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed. When destinations are managed via environment variables, the write is rejected with 409 and nothing is created; reads still return the current values.

post.summary: Send a test message to a log streaming destination
post.description: Send a test message to the destination to verify it is reachable and configured correctly. Requires the `eventBusDestination:test` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the log streaming destination.

get.description: Retrieve a single log streaming destination by id. Requires the `eventBusDestination:read` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the log streaming destination.

put.description: Replace an existing log streaming destination. The update takes effect exactly as it would from the UI, using the same validation. Requires the `eventBusDestination:update` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed. When destinations are managed via environment variables, the write is rejected with 409 and nothing is changed; reads still return the current values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the log streaming destination.

delete.description: Remove a log streaming destination. Requires the `eventBusDestination:delete` scope and the Log Streaming feature to be licensed. When destinations are managed via environment variables, the delete is rejected with 409 and nothing is removed; reads still return the current values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the log streaming destination.

personalSpacePublishing.description: Whether members may publish workflows and agents from their personal space.

personalSpaceSharing.description: Whether members may share workflows and credentials from their personal space.

publishedPersonalWorkflowsCount.description: Number of currently published personal workflows, shown for awareness before tightening the policy.

sharedPersonalWorkflowsCount.description: Number of personal workflows currently shared with other users.

sharedPersonalCredentialsCount.description: Number of personal credentials currently shared with other users.

personalSpacePublishing.description: Whether members may publish workflows and agents from their personal space.

personalSpaceSharing.description: Whether members may share workflows and credentials from their personal space.

publishedPersonalWorkflowsCount.description: Read-only usage count returned by GET. Ignored on write so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body.

sharedPersonalWorkflowsCount.description: Read-only usage count returned by GET. Ignored on write so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body.

sharedPersonalCredentialsCount.description: Read-only usage count returned by GET. Ignored on write so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body.

SecurityPolicyUpdate.description: Full security policy. All writable fields must be provided; partial updates are not supported.

metadata.description: Identity provider metadata in XML format. Redacted on read when set because it contains IdP certificates; never echoed back in plaintext. Use an empty string when unset.

metadataUrl.description: URL to fetch identity provider metadata from. Use an empty string when unset.

ignoreSSL.description: Whether to ignore SSL certificate errors when fetching metadata from a URL.

loginLabel.description: Label shown on the SAML login button.

wantMessageSigned.description: Whether signed SAML messages are required.

signingPrivateKey.description: PEM-encoded private key for signing SAML AuthnRequests. Redacted on read when set; never echoed back in plaintext. Use an empty string when unset.

signingCertificate.description: PEM-encoded certificate containing the public key matching the signing private key. Redacted on read when set; never echoed back in plaintext. Use an empty string when unset.

relayState.description: Default relay state value for SAML requests. Use an empty string when unset.

metadata.description: Identity provider metadata in XML format. Use an empty string to clear stored metadata (also clears metadataUrl when no URL is provided). Use the redaction placeholder from a prior GET to leave an existing value unchanged.

metadataUrl.description: URL to fetch identity provider metadata from. Use an empty string to clear a stored URL.

ignoreSSL.description: Whether to ignore SSL certificate errors when fetching metadata from a URL.

loginLabel.description: Label shown on the SAML login button.

wantMessageSigned.description: Whether signed SAML messages are required.

signingPrivateKey.description: PEM-encoded private key for signing SAML AuthnRequests. Use an empty string to clear an existing key, or the redaction placeholder from a prior GET to leave it unchanged.

signingCertificate.description: PEM-encoded certificate containing the public key matching the signing private key. Use an empty string when unused or to clear an existing certificate.

relayState.description: Default relay state value for SAML requests. Use an empty string when unused.

entityID.description: Service provider entity ID. Returned by GET for convenience; ignored on write so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body.

returnUrl.description: Assertion Consumer Service return URL. Returned by GET for convenience; ignored on write so a GET response can be sent back as a PUT body.

SamlConfigurationUpdate.description: Full SAML SSO configuration. Every field must be provided; use empty strings or empty arrays when a value is unset. Partial updates are not supported.

isGlobal.description: Whether the credential is available for use by all users.

isResolvable.description: Whether the credential can be dynamically resolved by a resolver.

resolvableAllowFallback.description: Whether the credential resolver may fall back to static credentials if dynamic resolution fails.

resolverId.description: ID of the dynamic credential resolver associated with this credential, if any.

role.description: Role of the credential in this project (e.g. credential:owner)

createdAt.description: When the credential was shared with this project

updatedAt.description: When the sharing was last updated

resolvableAllowFallback.description: Whether the credential resolver may fall back to static credentials if dynamic resolution fails.

Paginate through tags by setting the cursor parameter to a nextCursor attribute returned by a previous request. Default value fetches the first "page" of the collection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the node group

name.description: Display name of the node group

description.description: Optional plain-text description of the node group

nodeIds.description: IDs of the nodes that belong to this group

errorWorkflow.description: The ID of the workflow that contains the error trigger node.

callerIds.description: Comma-separated list of workflow IDs allowed to call this workflow (only used with workflowsFromAList policy)

timeSavedPerExecution.description: Estimated time saved per execution in minutes

availableInMCP.description:
"Controls whether this workflow is accessible via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
Defaults to false.

When enabled, this workflow can be called by MCP clients (AI assistants and other tools
that support MCP). This allows external AI tools to discover and execute this workflow
as part of their capabilities.

Requirements for enabling MCP access:
- The workflow must be active (not deactivated)
- The workflow must contain at least one active Webhook node
- Only webhook-triggered workflows can be exposed via MCP

Security note: When a workflow is available in MCP, it can be discovered and executed
by any MCP client that has the appropriate API credentials for your n8n instance.
"

id.description: Server-generated unique id of the destination. Returned in responses; not accepted in requests.

label.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI.

enabled.description: Whether the destination currently receives events.

subscribedEvents.description: Event names (or group prefixes, e.g. `n8n.workflow`, which matches all `n8n.workflow.*` events) this destination receives. Retrieve the full list of streamable event names from `GET /settings/log-streaming/event-types`.

anonymizeAuditMessages.description: Whether audit message payloads are anonymized before being sent.

"Syslog facility code (0–23, per RFC 5424). Common values: 0 Kernel, 1 User, 3 System, 13 Audit, 14 Alert, 16 Local0, 17 Local1, 18 Local2, 19 Local3, 20 Local4, 21 Local5, 22 Local6, 23 Local7.
"

Destination.description:
"A log streaming destination. The `type` field selects the variant and its type-specific fields: `webhook`, `syslog`, or `sentry`.
"

success.description: Whether the test message was delivered to the destination successfully.

updatedAt.description: Last time the role was updated.

shared.description: Shared entries (project id, name, role, createdAt, updatedAt) from the credential's shared relation

ImportBlockingIssueOneOf1.description: A workflow whose source id already matches one in the target project, under the `fail` conflict policy.

existingProjectId.description: Project that owns the existing workflow, or null when no owning project could be determined.

isArchived.description: Whether the existing workflow is archived.

ImportBlockingIssueOneOf2.description: A `source`-policy workflow whose id is already taken on the instance. Workflow ids are globally unique, so the id cannot be created in the target project. The existing workflow may live in any project — including the target project itself — and may be archived (archived workflows keep their id but are never matched for updates).

existingParentFolderId.description: Folder that currently contains the matched workflow, or null when it lives at the project root.

targetFolderId.description: Folder the import was requested to land in.

ImportBlockingIssueOneOf3.description: A workflow whose source id already matches one in the target project but lives outside the requested import folder. Folder-targeted imports cannot update workflows in place at a different location.

existingParentFolderId.description: "For `parent-mismatch`: the matched folder's current parent in the target."

expectedParentFolderId.description: "For `parent-mismatch`: the parent the package would place the folder under."

existingProjectId.description: "For `id-in-other-project`: the project that already owns the id."

ImportBlockingIssueOneOf4.description:
"A package folder that cannot be imported as-is. `kind` distinguishes the cause: `parent-mismatch` (a folder matched by id sits under a different parent than the package places it), `id-in-other-project` (the folder id already exists in a different project — ids are globally unique), or `fail-policy` (the folder already exists and `folderConflictPolicy` is `fail`).
"

targetId.description: Target credential id for an explicit credential binding.

expectedType.description:
"For `type_mismatch`: the credential type the package's workflow node requires.
"

actualType.description:
"For `type_mismatch`: the actual type of the resolved target credential.
"

ImportBlockingIssueOneOf5.description: A credential reference that could not be resolved in the target project.

sections.title: Credentials not used in any workflow
sections.description: These credentials are not used in any workflow. Keeping unused credentials in your instance is an unneeded security risk.
sections.recommendation: Consider deleting these credentials if you no longer need them.

sections.title: Expressions in "Execute Query" fields in SQL nodes
sections.description: This SQL node has an expression in the "Query" field of an "Execute Query" operation. Building a SQL query with an expression may lead to a SQL injection attack.
sections.recommendation: Consider using the "Query Parameters" field to pass parameters to the query

sections.title: Nodes that interact with the filesystem
sections.description: This node reads from and writes to any accessible file in the host filesystem. Sensitive file content may be manipulated through a node operation.
sections.recommendation: Consider protecting any sensitive files in the host filesystem

sections.title: Community nodes
sections.description: This node is sourced from the community. Community nodes are not vetted by the n8n team and have full access to the host system.
sections.recommendation: Consider reviewing the source code in any community nodes installed in this n8n instance

sections.title: Unprotected webhooks in instance
sections.description: These webhook nodes have the "Authentication" field set to "None" and are not directly connected to a node to validate the payload. Every unprotected webhook allows your workflow to be called by any third party who knows the webhook URL.
sections.recommendation: Consider setting the "Authentication" field to an option other than "None"

UpsertRowRequestFilter.description: Filter conditions to match existing row. If no row matches, a new row is inserted.

maxFailures.description: Maximum failures within the sliding window before the breaker opens.

failureWindow.description: Sliding window, in milliseconds, over which failures are counted.

DestinationCommonCircuitBreaker.description: Circuit-breaker tuning for delivery. After repeated failures the destination stops sending for a cool-down window instead of hammering an unhealthy target. All fields are optional; sensible defaults apply when omitted.

allowUnauthorizedCerts.description: Accept self-signed or otherwise invalid TLS certificates.

isRedacted.description: Whether the execution data was redacted.

canReveal.description: Whether the current user has permission to reveal the redacted data.

keepAlive.description: Whether to keep sockets open for reuse.

maxSockets.description: Maximum sockets per host kept open at once.

maxFreeSockets.description: Maximum idle sockets per host kept open.

maxRedirects.description: Maximum number of redirects to follow.

host.description: Proxy host, without protocol or port.

destinationProjectId.description: The ID of the project to transfer the credential to.

loadWorkflow.description: Whether to load the currently saved workflow to execute instead of the one saved at the time of the execution. If set to true, it will retry with the latest version of the workflow.

status.description: Array of execution statuses to stop. Must include at least one status.

workflowId.description: Optional workflow ID to filter executions. If not provided, will stop executions across all accessible workflows.

startedAfter.description: Only stop executions that started after this time.

startedBefore.description: Only stop executions that started before this time.

stopped.description: The number of executions that were successfully stopped.

versionId.description: The specific version ID to activate or publish. If not provided, the latest version is used.

name.description: Optional name for the workflow version during activation.

description.description: Optional description for the workflow version during activation.

destinationProjectId.description: The ID of the project to transfer the workflow to.

oneOf.description: Updated rows when returnData is true

deletedCount.description: The number of rows that were deleted

oneOf.description: Deleted rows when returnData is true

relations.description: A list of userIds and roles to add to the project.

role.description: The role assigned to the user in the project.

version.description: Specific semver version to update to

verify.description: Whether to verify the package against the n8n-vetted package list. Setting to false will allow installing or updating to an unverified version. Default is true.

projectId.description: Target project id. Omit or send empty to import into the caller's personal project.

folderId.description: Optional folder within the target project. Omit or send empty for project root.

bindings.description:
"Optional JSON object of explicit source→target id bindings, keyed by entity type. Only `credentials` is supported today: send `{\"credentials\":{\"<packageCredentialId>\":\"<targetCredentialId>\"}}` to map credential ids from the package to credential ids on the target instance. These explicit bindings are validated on type and applied before `credentialMatchingMode` resolution runs.
"

folders.description: Folder shells created or skipped in the target project.

projects.description: Project shells created or matched-and-updated. Present for project packages; empty when importing loose workflows/folders.

value.description: Array of inserted row IDs (when returnType is 'id')
Array of inserted rows (when returnType is 'all')

daysAbandonedWorkflow.description: Days for a workflow to be considered abandoned if not executed

role.description: The role assigned to the user in the project.

sourceWorkflowId.description: Workflow id as it appeared in the package.

localId.description:
"Workflow id on the target instance: newly assigned for `created` (fresh under `workflowIdPolicy=new`, the package id under `source`), the existing workflow's id for `updated`/`skipped`.
"

activeVersionId.description: Published version on the target instance, if any. `null` when the workflow is not published after import.

sourceFolderId.description: Folder id as it appeared in the package.

localId.description: Folder id on the target instance (equal to `sourceFolderId`; folder ids are reused).

parentFolderId.description: Resolved parent folder on the target, or `null` at the project root.

sourceProjectId.description: Project id as it appeared in the package.

localId.description: Project id on the target instance (equal to `sourceProjectId`; project ids are reused).

matched.description: Source credential ids from the package that matched existing credentials on the target instance.

stubbed.description: Source credential ids for which empty placeholder credentials were created in the target project.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseCredenti.description: Source credential ids grouped by whether they matched an existing credential or were created as stubs. Full source→target id mapping is in `bindings.credentials`.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseBindings.description: Source id → target id mappings produced during import, one map per entity type. Each value maps an id as it appeared in the package to the id on the target instance.

GetDiscover200ResponseDataFilters.description: Available query parameter filters. The values arrays reflect what the caller's scopes permit.

error.description: Present when `state` is `failed`. Explains why publish or unpublish could not be applied.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseWorkflow.description: Outcome of applying the selected publishing policy to this workflow.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseBindings.description: Credential id from `requirements.credentials` in the package manifest → matched credential id on the target instance.

GetDiscover200ResponseDataResourcesAddit.description: Request body schema (only present when include=schemas and the endpoint accepts a request body).

WorkflowSettingsCallerPolicy.description:
"Controls which workflows are allowed to call this workflow using the Execute Workflow node.
Defaults to workflowsFromSameOwner.

Available options:
- `any`: Any workflow can call this workflow (no restrictions)
- `none`: No other workflows can call this workflow (completely blocked)
- `workflowsFromSameOwner` (default): Only workflows owned by the same project can call this workflow
  * For personal projects: Only workflows created by the same user
  * For team projects: Only workflows within the same team project
- `workflowsFromAList`: Only specific workflows listed in the `callerIds` field can call this workflow
  * Requires the `callerIds` field to specify which workflow IDs are allowed
  * See `callerIds` field documentation for usage
"

WorkflowSettingsRedactionPolicy.description:
"Controls whether execution data is redacted for this workflow.

Available options:
- `none` (default): No redaction — all execution data is stored.
- `non-manual`: Redact production (non-manually triggered) executions only.
- `manual-only`: Redact manually triggered executions only.
- `all`: Redact all executions (manual and production).

When the instance has a redaction floor configured, the policy must be equal to
or stricter than the floor. A policy weaker than the floor is rejected with 422
on create, and on update when it changes the stored policy; an unchanged
pre-existing below-floor policy is preserved (the floor is not applied
retroactively). Omitting this field when creating a workflow seeds it to the
instance floor instead.
"

TestRunSummaryFinalResult.description: Overall result of the run, derived from its test cases once completed.

PullAutoPublish.description:
"Controls automatic workflow publishing after import:
- `none`: Keep workflows in their local published state (default)
- `all`: Publish all imported workflows
- `published`: Publish only workflows that were published locally before import
"

InsertRowsRequestReturnType.description:
"- count: Return only the number of rows inserted
- id: Return an array of inserted row IDs
- all: Return the full row data for all inserted rows
"

ExportPackageRequestMissingWorkflowDepen.description: Policy for missing static sub-workflow dependencies. Currently only `fail` is supported; `reference-only` and `include-in-package` are reserved for upcoming export modes.

DestinationWebhookType.description: Sends each event as an HTTP request to a URL.

DestinationSyslogType.description: Sends each event to a syslog server.

DestinationSentryType.description: Sends each event to a Sentry project.

SecurityPolicyRedactionEnforcementFloor.description: Minimum execution-data redaction level enforced across the instance.

SecurityPolicyUpdateRedactionEnforcement.description: Minimum execution-data redaction level enforced across the instance.

DestinationWebhookAllOf2OptionsQueryPara.description: How array-valued query parameters are serialized into the query string.

PostN8NPackagesImportFormDataRequestCred.description: How credential references in `requirements.credentials` are matched on the target instance. `id-only` (default) matches by id. `name-and-type` matches credentials with the exact same name and type. `type-only` matches any credential of the same type. For `name-and-type` and `type-only`, candidates are ranked by scope — a credential owned by the target project wins over one merely shared into it, which in turn wins over a global credential; if several candidates remain in the winning scope, the most recently updated one is chosen.

What to do when a credential reference cannot be resolved. `create-stub` (default) creates empty credential placeholders in the target project for missing references. `must-preexist` requires every referenced credential to already exist.

PostN8NPackagesImportFormDataRequestWork.description: `new-version` updates matching workflows and creates a new version, `fail` rejects the import when any matching workflow exists, and `skip` leaves matching workflows unchanged.

Controls the id each newly created workflow receives. `new` (default) mints a fresh id and records the package id as `sourceWorkflowId`, so the same package can be imported repeatedly without id collisions. `source` reuses the package's own workflow id on the target instance. Workflows matched to an existing workflow in the target project (status `updated` or `skipped`) always keep that workflow's current id, regardless of policy.

Controls whether imported workflows are published after content is written. `preserve-published-state` keeps new workflows inactive and republishes an updated workflow only when it was already published and the package workflow is published too, so drafts are never published. `match-source` follows the package workflow's published flag. `publish-all` publishes every imported workflow. `unpublish-all` leaves new workflows inactive and unpublishes updated workflows that were published.

PostN8NPackagesImportFormDataRequestFold.description: Controls what happens when a package folder (matched by id in the target project) already exists at the same position. `merge` (default) reuses the existing folder as-is and merges the package's children into it; `fail` rejects the import. A folder whose id exists under a different parent, or belongs to another project, always blocks the import. Requires the `folder:create` scope and a license that supports folders when the package contains folders.

PostN8NPackagesImportFormDataRequestData.description: How data tables referenced by the package's workflows are matched against the target project. `by-id` matches the target-project table with the same id (imported tables keep their source id) and never falls back to name matching, so a match survives a rename on the target. Currently the only mode.

"Controls what happens when a referenced data table has no match in the target project. `create` (default) creates it from the package schema, keeping the source id, with no rows; requires the `dataTable:create` scope. `must-preexist` rejects the import. `do-nothing` skips creation of missing tables — matched tables are still validated for schema compatibility and can still block the import. Matched tables are always used as-is: never renamed, altered, or filled.
"

"How strictly a matched data table's schema is compared. `keep-existing` (default) accepts a target table that has every package column with the same name and type, even when the target table also has additional columns of its own. `fail` is the strict drift-detection choice: any difference, including such target-only columns, rejects the import. A missing package column or a type mismatch always rejects. Both policies are non-destructive — the matched target table is never altered, and package columns are never added to it.
"

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseWorkflow.description: Import outcome for this package workflow.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseFoldersI.description: Import outcome for this package folder.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseProjects.description: Import outcome for this package project.

PostN8NPackagesImport200ResponseWorkflow.description: `blocked` means the imported version could not be published and no version is active (for example because the workflow uses a stubbed credential). When a prior published version remains active, `state` is `unchanged` with `skippedPublishReason` instead. `failed` means publish or unpublish was attempted but did not succeed.

"Present when `state` is `blocked`: the imported version could not be published and no version is active.
"

"Present when `state` is `unchanged` but the policy wanted to publish the imported version: a prior published version is still active (for example after an update with stubbed credentials).
"

badRequest.description: The request is invalid or provides malformed data.

notFound.description: The specified resource was not found.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: The maximum number of items to return.

cursor.name: cursor
cursor.description: Paginate by setting the cursor parameter to the nextCursor attribute returned by the previous request's response. Default value fetches the first "page" of the collection. See pagination for more detail.

includeData.name: includeData
includeData.description: Whether or not to include the execution's detailed data.

redactExecutionData.name: redactExecutionData
redactExecutionData.description: Controls execution data redaction. When `true`, execution output data is always redacted. When `false`, requests unredacted (revealed) data — requires the `execution:reveal` scope. When omitted, follows the workflow redaction policy.

testRunId.name: runId
testRunId.description: The ID of the test run.

offset.name: offset
offset.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

includeRole.name: includeRole
includeRole.description: Whether to include the user's role or not.

userIdentifier.name: id
userIdentifier.description: The ID or email of the user.

dataTableId.name: dataTableId
dataTableId.description: The ID of the data table
